title: Wild Scotland
creator: John Duncan
award_or_showcase_provenance: New York City Drone Film Festival (NYCDFF) 2016 (2nd Annual) -- Landscape Award winner
notes: MANUALLY CURATED WORKED EXAMPLE -- one exemplar only, per spec AC3.4/Scope-out (no automated scraping/bulk downloading was performed to produce this record). Award verified this session via the festival's own site: a WebFetch of https://www.nycdronefilmfestival.com/winners returned a per-year/per-category winners listing that includes '2016 -- Landscape: "Wild Scotland" by John Duncan' (alongside that year's separate Architecture winner, 'Greystone Rising' by Jody Johnson) -- i.e. this is a directly-sourced primary-source category win, not an inferred/elimination-based attribution. This was independently corroborated by web search of john-duncan.co.uk (a page titled 'Wild Scotland wins New York Drone Festival', though a direct WebFetch of that page failed with a TLS 'self signed certificate' error in this session so its content could not be independently re-extracted here) plus secondary press coverage (PetaPixel 'This Aerial Short Film Shows the Beauty of Scotland's Wilderness at Golden Hour', TwistedSifter 'An Aerial Tour Through the Scotland Wilderness', Cinematic Poems) describing 'Wild Scotland' as John Duncan's 2015 aerial short filmed with a DJI Inspire 1 / DJI Phantom 3 across the Scottish Highlands, Islands, John O'Groats and North Berwick, and noting it also appeared on National Geographic's 'Short Film Showcase' and earned a Vimeo Staff Pick. `source_url` points to the upload on John Duncan's own YouTube channel (listed there as 'Scotland Aerial Filming - Wild Scotland') rather than the National Geographic re-post (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=KE8-flJJNT0, uploaded under NatGeo's own channel/branding) or the original Vimeo upload (https://vimeo.com/146742515, found via web search but whose oEmbed endpoint returned 404 and whose page was blocked by a Vimeo bot/security-verification wall in this session, so it could not be independently verified here) -- this session verified the YouTube URL directly via the oEmbed endpoint (https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sTy0TNEBlNg&format=json), which returned title 'Scotland Aerial Filming - Wild Scotland' and author_name/author_url 'John Duncan' / https://www.youtube.com/@johnduncan4887 -- i.e. the creator field is confirmed by the platform's own oEmbed metadata, matching the festival-listed winner name. `title` is recorded as 'Wild Scotland' (the film's canonical title per the festival listing and all press coverage) even though the specific YouTube upload's on-channel title string is the slightly longer 'Scotland Aerial Filming - Wild Scotland'; a full JS-rendered fetch of the video's description/metadata to confirm this is the complete festival-winning cut (rather than an alternate edit) was not possible in this session (WebFetch of the watch page returned only static footer boilerplate, no video metadata) -- treat the video-vs-festival-cut correspondence as well-corroborated by title/creator/subject match but not frame-by-frame confirmed. `license_category` is set to the conservative default 'all-rights-reserved': no explicit Creative Commons marking on this specific upload was verified in this session, so per this project's stated default (assume all-rights-reserved unless the uploader's license setting is directly confirmed otherwise), `local_media_path` stays null and no video file was downloaded or persisted anywhere in this repo. `duration` is left null: YouTube's oEmbed response does not include a duration field, and no YouTube Data API call was made in this session -- rather than fabricate a plausible-looking number (press coverage mentions '3.5-minute' in prose for what may be this same film, but this was not independently confirmed against this exact upload), this field is left unset pending a real API call or manual playback confirmation (see reference_pack/README.md's refresh/expiry process). `scores` (sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness) are `scores_provenance: manually_estimated` placeholder judgments based on the video's known genre, era, and reputation (2015-era cinematic aerial landscape footage shot on DJI Inspire 1/Phantom 3, festival Landscape-category winner, Vimeo Staff Pick, NatGeo showcase pickup) -- deliberately set slightly below the most modern 4K/8K-era exemplars in this pack to reflect its older camera/gimbal generation, NOT measurements from Capability 1's actual scoring functions (scoring_sharpness.py/scoring_exposure.py/scoring_motion_smoothness.py), since no source file was ever downloaded to run those functions against, per the same no-bulk-download constraint. `composite_score` IS a real computation: it was produced this session by calling this repo's own `drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.composite.compute_composite_score` with these three manually-estimated inputs (sharpness=0.75, exposure=0.78, motion_smoothness=0.8, composition=None) and `drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.weights.default_weights()` (weights_version 'default-v1', equal 1/3 weighting on sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness, composition weight fixed at 0.0) via `.venv/bin/python`, yielding composite_score=0.7766666666666666 (rounded to 0.7767 here) -- so the composite arithmetic itself is genuine and reproducible, even though its inputs are estimates rather than measurements.
